own_review_points: Lifecycle.java isStopped() javadoc is a copy-paste error: '@return true, if this object is started' (says 'started' for the stopped predicate)
BlockingLifecycle.NONE returns false for BOTH isStarted() and isStopped(), so the started-xor-stopped invariant can be violated
Lifecycle default isStopped()=!isStarted() reports stopped during STARTING/STOPPING states; real impls override it, default is semantically wrong

ServiceValueRegistry.capture: ServiceInstaller.BlockingBuilder.of(dependency) already calls .requires(dependency) internally, so the explicit .requires(dependency) is redundant (verified against of(ServiceDependency) factory)
BlockingLifecycle.compose: stop() only fires on STARTED->STOPPING; a service stopped while STARTING is in-flight may not be stopped (minor race)
Human nit: RequestControllerSubsystemTestCase could use BlockingBuilder.<T>of(supplier).provides(...).build() instead of map(Function.identity()) to avoid building DefaultBlockingBuilder twice
notes: Large PR (28 files) truncated at ~150k. v8's two headline points (CapabilityServiceInstaller DefaultBlockingBuilder default-mode regression ON_DEMAND->PASSIVE, and lost blocking() instrumentation method) cite the subsystem-module CapabilityServiceInstaller.java DEFINITION, which is past the truncation marker (only caller usages are visible) — scored unverifiable=0 (plausible and detailed, but cannot confirm). Verified good points: the Lifecycle isStopped javadoc copy-paste error, the BlockingLifecycle.NONE invariant oddity, the default-isStopped semantic issue, and — the best catch — the redundant .requires(dependency) (confirmed of(ServiceDependency) does of(provider).requires(dependency)). The warnf log nit is trivial. Missed the human's optional builder nit.
